name: Juan's Killer Flan
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. In a large bowl, mix together the eggs, sweetened condensed milk, evaporated milk, 1 cup of sugar, vanilla extract, and lemon juice. Blend until no solids remain and the sugar is evenly distributed.
3. If desired, add a small amount of Amaretto or Bailey's Irish Cream or softened cream cheese for extra flavor.
4. In a meat loaf or bread mold, pour in the remaining 1 cup of sugar and water to create a syrupy mixture.
5. Place the mold in the oven and heat on high until it starts to turn a medium caramel color. Stir occasionally to ensure even heating.
6. Remove the mold from the oven when the syrup reaches the desired color. Allow it to cool slightly.
7. Pour the flan mixture into the cooled syrup, ensuring the entire surface is covered twice with aluminum foil.
8. Place the mold in a larger pan (double boiler) and fill the middle with water so that it almost reaches the edge of the mold.
9. Seal the top of the mold tightly to prevent steam or hot air from escaping; otherwise, the flan will not set properly and may appear grainy on the outside.
10. Set the oven to BAKE and cook for 1 hour. Turn off the heat and allow the flan to cool completely in the pan.
11. Once cooled, remove the flan from the larger pan and refrigerate until ready to serve. The recipe serves about 8 people but can last up to three days when stored properly in the refrigerator.
